name: APEX//CONTROL — Tactical HUD

contents.content:
{% comment %} Recreation of the built-in docker service home page: status header, the Logs/Terminal/Details tab area, then the metrics grid. Edit freely from here. Tokens self-gate on permissions; the {% if Permissions.* %} wrappers remove empty slots like the built-in page. Note: both terminals connect as soon as the page loads (the built-in page connects them on first tab click). {% endcomment %}
{% component "docker-status-header" %}
{% if Permissions.LogConsole %}{% endif %} {% if Permissions.Console %}{% endif %}
{% if Permissions.LogConsole %}
{% endif %} {% if Permissions.Console %}
{% endif %}
{% if Permissions.LogConsole %}
{% component "docker-logs-terminal" %}
{% endif %} {% if Permissions.Console %}
{% component "docker-exec-terminal" %}
{% endif %}
{% component "service-details" %}
{% if Permissions.LiveStats %}
{% if Permissions.CpuStats %}
{% component "service-cpu-tile" %}
{% endif %} {% if Permissions.MemoryStats %}
{% component "service-memory-tile" %}
{% endif %}
{% component "service-disk-tile" %}
{% component "service-uptime-tile" %}
{% endif %}

{% comment %} Recreation of the built-in game service header: title block, connection info cards, power buttons. This strip replaces the compiled header on EVERY page of the service (Console, File Manager, ...), not just Home. Edit freely - or make it a single empty
to hide the header entirely. MudBlazor's stock d-none d-md-flex hides the cards on phones (same breakpoint the built-in header uses); template CSS cannot declare @media rules in sandboxed themes, so use it. {% endcomment %}
{% component "service-title" %}
{% component "game-connection-info" %}
{% component "service-power" %}

{% component "announcements" %}
